title: Reconciliation Correction — Investment Accounts (Revaluation)
description: When a reconciliation discrepancy is detected on an Investment account, the system shall allow the user to create a REVALUATION transaction. A positive discrepancy (current market value > last recorded value) represents unrealized capital gain. A negative discrepancy represents unrealized capital loss. The transaction amount equals the absolute discrepancy value, signed appropriately. Revaluation transactions are stored as a distinct type and displayed separately from regular Income/Expense entries in the timeline.
acceptance_criteria: Revaluation is only offered for account type = Investment; non-investment accounts use FR-011 instead
Revaluation transaction is pre-filled with the absolute discrepancy amount and labeled 'Revaluation'
Positive revaluation (gain) and negative revaluation (loss) are visually distinguishable in the timeline
Revaluation transactions are excluded from expense/income category reports by default (they are not real cash flows)
After saving, querying the account balance returns the user-entered actual market value
Multiple revaluations over time form a history of investment value development visible in the timeline
